fresh_facts.claim: Israeli strikes on March 18 hit Tehran-area headquarters, ballistic missile sites, regime infrastructure, Basij positions, and Internal Security Forces logistics.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: On March 17, Israeli strikes killed Basij Commander Brigadier General Gholamreza Soleimani and deputy Ghassem Ghoureishi at a makeshift headquarters in Tehran.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: Approximately 300 Basij commanders were reportedly killed overnight March 16-17 in Israeli strikes.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli strikes on March 17 targeted the 7th Artesh Airbase in Shiraz, damaging C-130s and Il-76 aircraft.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli strikes on March 17 targeted an ammunition depot in Sirjan and a missile site in Tabriz.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli strikes on March 17 hit the IRGC Navy headquarters in Tehran, which was used for operations against Israel.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli strikes on March 17 targeted multiple Basij checkpoints and bases in Tehran, including Kamraniyeh and the Imam Hadi unit.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli strikes on March 17 targeted the IRGC Quds Force Imam Ali unit, which is used for proxy training.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli strikes on March 17 targeted the Fars Law Enforcement Command headquarters.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: Combined US-Israeli strikes targeted Kataib Hezbollah positions in Jurf al Sakhr starting March 16 at 3:00 PM ET.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on military operations

fresh_facts.claim: US-Israeli strikes hit PMF Brigade 12 headquarters near Baghdad, injuring 3 personnel.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on military operations

fresh_facts.claim: The USS George H. W. Bush was deployed to the Middle East on March 7.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on US military deployments

fresh_facts.claim: US CENTCOM conducted strikes on a Tehran drone facility on March 11.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on US military operations

fresh_facts.claim: US CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper stated on March 16 that focus has shifted to Iran's manufacturing apparatus.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on US military statements

fresh_facts.claim: Iran launched a missile barrage on March 17 following Israeli strikes, killing 2 Israeli civilians in Ramat Gan.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian military response

fresh_facts.claim: Reports indicate Iran has approximately 1,000 ballistic missiles remaining after a week of hostilities.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian military capabilities

fresh_facts.claim: The Iranian regime announced that Mojtaba Khamenei has been introduced as the new leader.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian leadership transitions

fresh_facts.claim: Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first message as new leader emphasizing war continuation, opening new fronts, and Basij street presence.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian leadership statements

fresh_facts.claim: Mojtaba Khamenei's inner circle is dominated by hardline IRGC commanders.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian leadership composition

fresh_facts.claim: Ali Larijani, a top Iranian figure, was assassinated as reported on March 18.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian leadership deaths

fresh_facts.claim: The Iranian regime claims its political system remains intact despite assassinations of top figures.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian regime statements

fresh_facts.claim: Reports describe IRGC checkpoints and anti-IRGC chants in Tehran neighborhoods such as Saadat Abad as of March 15.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on internal Iranian unrest

fresh_facts.claim: The Iranian regime judiciary is preparing 1988-style measures against protesters.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian regime repression

fresh_facts.claim: A UN Fact-Finding Mission issued a warning of crimes against humanity in Shiraz prior to March 15.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on UN findings

fresh_facts.claim: The Albanian Prime Minister planned to call for an anti-Iran terrorism resolution vote as of March 13.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on international diplomatic actions

fresh_facts.claim: Iran attacked Gulf states with threats and interceptions as reported in past 24 hours on March 18.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian regional actions

fresh_facts.claim: No ground invasion of Iran has been reported as of March 18.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on military operations

fresh_facts.claim: Iran's nuclear stance remained unchanged as of March 18.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian nuclear policy

regime_fall.current_status: Following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death and Mojtaba Khamenei's succession, the Iranian regime faces an unprecedented convergence of external and internal pressures: intensive coordinated US-Israeli military operations have systematically targeted Iranian military command structures, with Israeli strikes on March 17-18 killing approximately 300+ Basij commanders including senior leadership, destroying critical infrastructure, and degrading command-and-control capabilities, while internally the regime deploys expanded Basij forces and judicial repression measures against anti-IRGC protests and unrest in Tehran neighborhoods.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli strikes on March 17-18 killed senior Basij leadership including Commander Brigadier General Gholamreza Soleimani and approximately 300 additional Basij commanders, while simultaneously targeting IRGC headquarters, Navy headquarters, missile sites, airbases, and ammunition depots across Iran, representing the most comprehensive targeting of regime military command structures in the campaign.
